summary: Good soundboard recording with thick Brent Mydland keys and a decent Miracle→Bertha transition despite minor crunchiness
review: The tape captures the characteristic full‑bodied keyboard tone of the mid‑80s era, which the reviewer praises. The show is viewed positively overall, though the transition from "Miracle" into "Bertha" is described as a bit crunchy. Recording quality is solid and enjoyable.

ai_rating.rationale: Only one review; it rates the show 4 stars and notes good keyboard sound with minor transition issues, indicating a solid but not exceptional performance and recording.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Thick keyboard tone praised; overall clear. Minor critique of the Miracle→Bertha transition suggests a small mixing/segment glitch.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Transition from "Miracle" into "Bertha" is noted as crunchy, but no major flow problems are mentioned.

band_member_comments.Keys: Thick, full‑bodied keyboard sound praised (Brent Mydland era).
